content.text: Blueprint Definitions

DEPRECATED: Azure Blueprints retired July 11, 2026. This topic covers the Blueprint Definitions concept for teams managing existing assignments. For new environments, use Azure Deployment Stacks — see the migration section below.

An Azure Blueprint definition is a container that combines four types of artifacts — resource groups, ARM templates, policy assignments, and role assignments — into a single deployable unit. When assigned to a subscription, all artifacts are deployed atomically. Blueprint definitions are stored at a management group or subscription scope; management group scope is preferred because one definition can be assigned to any child subscription without copying it. Definitions exist in draft state until explicitly published; once published, a version is immutable.

attrs.alt: Blueprint definition composition: four artifact types, lifecycle stages, and migration path to Deployment Stacks

content.text: Blueprint Definition Structure

A blueprint definition has three top-level properties: targetScope (subscription or managementGroup), parameters (blueprint-level parameters that artifact parameters can reference), and resourceGroups (placeholder resource group names with optional location). The resource group placeholders are resolved at assignment time — the actual resource group is created in the target subscription with the name and location you provide when assigning. This lets one definition serve many subscriptions with different naming conventions.
Property
Purpose
Example
targetScope
Whether the blueprint assigns to a subscription or management group
subscription (most common — policies and role assignments are subscription-scoped)
parameters
Blueprint-level inputs exposed to all artifacts
subscriptionLocation, logAnalyticsWorkspaceId, appTeamGroupObjectId
resourceGroups
Placeholder RG names resolved at assignment time
rg-workloads, rg-monitoring — created in target sub with specified location
name
Immutable definition identifier
landing-zone-baseline
displayName
Human-readable name shown in portal
Landing Zone Baseline v1.0
Creating a Blueprint Definition

Blueprint definitions are created via az blueprint create. The definition starts in draft state and accepts artifact additions. The targetScope must be subscription — management-group-scoped target scope deploys artifacts at management group level, which is rarely correct since ARM templates and role assignments typically target subscriptions. Store the definition at an MG scope (--management-group) so one definition services multiple subscriptions; this is different from targetScope, which controls where artifacts are deployed.
Adding Artifacts

Three artifact types attach to a blueprint definition: ARM template artifacts (az blueprint artifact template create) deploy Azure resources, policy assignment artifacts (az blueprint artifact policy create) assign Azure Policy definitions, and role assignment artifacts (az blueprint artifact role create) grant RBAC roles. Each artifact references the blueprint-level parameters via the [parameters("paramName")] token. Artifact dependencies are expressed with --depends-on — resource group artifacts must complete before ARM templates that deploy into them.

# Add artifacts to a blueprint definition
# Artifacts are the actual resources/policies/roles the blueprint deploys.

MG_ID='alz-landing-zones'
BP_NAME='landing-zone-baseline'

# Artifact 1: ARM template — deploy a storage account for diagnostic logs
az blueprint artifact template create \
  --blueprint-name $BP_NAME \
  --management-group $MG_ID \
  --artifact-name 'diag-storage-account' \
  --display-name 'Diagnostic Storage Account' \
  --resource-group 'rg-monitoring' \
  --depends-on 'rg-monitoring' \
  --template '{
    "$schema": "https://schema.management.azure.com/schemas/2019-04-01/deploymentTemplate.json#",
    "contentVersion": "1.0.0.0",
    "parameters": {
      "location": {"type": "string"}
    },
    "resources": [{
      "type": "Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts",
      "apiVersion": "2023-01-01",
      "name": "[concat(\"stdiag\", uniqueString(resourceGroup().id))]",
      "location": "[parameters(\"location\")]",
      "sku": {"name": "Standard_GRS"},
      "kind": "StorageV2",
      "properties": {"minimumTlsVersion": "TLS1_2"}
    }]
  }' \
  --parameters '{
    "location": {"value": "[parameters(\"subscriptionLocation\")]"}'

# Artifact 2: Policy assignment — enforce tag on resource groups
az blueprint artifact policy create \
  --blueprint-name $BP_NAME \
  --management-group $MG_ID \
  --artifact-name 'require-env-tag' \
  --display-name 'Require Environment tag on Resource Groups' \
  --policy-definition-id '/providers/Microsoft.Authorization/policyDefinitions/96670d01-0a4d-4649-9c89-2d3abc0a5025' \
  --parameters '{
    "tagName": {"value": "Environment"}
  }'

# Artifact 3: Role assignment — Contributor for app team group
az blueprint artifact role create \
  --blueprint-name $BP_NAME \
  --management-group $MG_ID \
  --artifact-name 'app-team-contributor' \
  --display-name 'App Team Contributor' \
  --role-definition-id '/providers/Microsoft.Authorization/roleDefinitions/b24988ac-6180-42a0-ab88-20f7382dd24c' \
  --principal-ids '[parameters("appTeamGroupObjectId")]'

# List all artifacts attached to the blueprint
az blueprint artifact list \
  --blueprint-name $BP_NAME \
  --management-group $MG_ID \
  --query '[].{Name:name, Kind:kind, DisplayName:displayName}' -o table
Publishing and Assigning

Publishing converts a draft into an immutable versioned snapshot. The version string is arbitrary — use semver (1.0, 1.1, 2.0) to communicate the change magnitude. Once published, edit the draft (which starts as a copy of the last publish) and publish a new version for the next assignment round. At assignment, you select the version, target subscription, lock mode, and parameter values. Lock mode AllResourcesDoNotDelete prevents deletion of blueprint-managed resources; AllResourcesReadOnly prevents both modifications and deletions.

# Publish a blueprint version and assign it to a subscription

MG_ID='alz-landing-zones'
BP_NAME='landing-zone-baseline'
TARGET_SUB='<landing-zone-sub-id>'

# 1. Publish the blueprint — creates an immutable version
#    Once published, the definition cannot be edited. Create v1.1 for changes.
az blueprint publish \
  --blueprint-name $BP_NAME \
  --management-group $MG_ID \
  --version '1.0' \
  --change-notes 'Initial baseline: diag storage, env tag policy, app team RBAC'

# 2. Verify published version
az blueprint version list \
  --blueprint-name $BP_NAME \
  --management-group $MG_ID \
  --query '[].{Version:name, Published:status, ChangeNotes:changeNotes}' -o table

# 3. Assign the blueprint to a subscription
#    lock-mode: None | AllResourcesDoNotDelete | AllResourcesReadOnly
az blueprint assignment create \
  --name 'assign-landing-zone-baseline' \
  --subscription $TARGET_SUB \
  --blueprint-version "/providers/Microsoft.Management/managementGroups/$MG_ID/providers/Microsoft.Blueprint/blueprints/$BP_NAME/versions/1.0" \
  --location eastus \
  --lock-mode AllResourcesDoNotDelete \
  --identity-type SystemAssigned \
  --parameters '{
    "subscriptionLocation": {"value": "eastus"},
    "logAnalyticsWorkspaceId": {"value": "/subscriptions/<mgmt-sub-id>/resourceGroups/rg-management-prod-eastus/providers/Microsoft.OperationalInsights/workspaces/log-management-prod-eastus"},
    "appTeamGroupObjectId": {"value": "<app-team-aad-group-oid>"}
  }'

# 4. Monitor assignment status
az blueprint assignment show \
  --name 'assign-landing-zone-baseline' \
  --subscription $TARGET_SUB \
  --query '{Status:provisioningState, Locks:locks.mode, Version:blueprintId}' -o json

# 5. Update to a new version (v1.1) after changes
az blueprint assignment update \
  --name 'assign-landing-zone-baseline' \
  --subscription $TARGET_SUB \
  --blueprint-version "/providers/Microsoft.Management/managementGroups/$MG_ID/providers/Microsoft.Blueprint/blueprints/$BP_NAME/versions/1.1"
Migration to Deployment Stacks

Azure Blueprints retired July 11, 2026. Existing assignments stop being enforced but do not delete deployed resources. The migration path: export artifact ARM templates, convert to Bicep with az bicep decompile, create a Deployment Stack (az stack sub create) with equivalent denySettings, then delete the blueprint assignment. Deployment Stacks replace blueprint lock modes with denySettings (DenyWriteAndDelete maps to ReadOnly; DenyDelete maps to DoNotDelete). Deployment Stacks have no versioning concept — use git tags on the Bicep file to track versions.

# Migrate Azure Blueprint assignments to Deployment Stacks
# Azure Blueprints retired July 11, 2026 — existing assignments stop being enforced.

# Step 1: List all active blueprint assignments in your tenant
az blueprint assignment list \
  --subscription '<your-sub-id>' \
  --query '[].{Name:name, Status:provisioningState, Blueprint:blueprintId}' -o table

# Step 2: Export the ARM template from the blueprint definition
#         This is the template artifact content inside the blueprint.
az blueprint artifact show \
  --blueprint-name 'landing-zone-baseline' \
  --management-group 'alz-landing-zones' \
  --artifact-name 'diag-storage-account' \
  --query 'template' -o json > diag_storage.json

# Step 3: Convert the ARM template to Bicep (optional but recommended)
az bicep decompile --file diag_storage.json
# Produces diag_storage.bicep — review and clean up the decompiled output

# Step 4: Create a Deployment Stack equivalent at subscription scope
#         Deployment Stacks replaces blueprint assignments.
az stack sub create \
  --name 'landing-zone-baseline-stack' \
  --location eastus \
  --template-file diag_storage.bicep \
  --parameters subscriptionLocation=eastus \
  --deny-settings-mode DenyWriteAndDelete \
  --deny-settings-apply-to-child-scopes \
  --action-on-unmanage deleteAll

# Step 5: Remove the blueprint assignment (does NOT delete deployed resources)
az blueprint assignment delete \
  --name 'assign-landing-zone-baseline' \
  --subscription '<your-sub-id>' \
  --yes

# Step 6: Verify Deployment Stack manages the resources
az stack sub show \
  --name 'landing-zone-baseline-stack' \
  --query '{Status:provisioningState, Resources:length(resources), DenySettings:denySettings.mode}' \
  -o json
Auditing Blueprint Assignments

Three Resource Graph queries cover the critical audit scenarios for blueprint retirement: list all active blueprint assignments with their lock mode (to identify which subscriptions need migration), find assignments not in succeeded state (stale or failed assignments), and identify assignments with active resource locks (these lose protection at retirement and must be migrated to Deployment Stack denySettings before the retirement date).

// Resource Graph: audit blueprint assignments and their status

// 1. List all blueprint assignments across all subscriptions
BlueprintResources
| where type =~ 'microsoft.blueprint/blueprintassignments'
| extend
    assignmentName = name,
    blueprintVersion = tostring(properties.blueprintId),
    provState = tostring(properties.provisioningState),
    lockMode = tostring(properties.locks.mode)
| project subscriptionId, assignmentName, blueprintVersion, provState, lockMode
| order by subscriptionId asc

// 2. Find blueprint assignments that are NOT in 'succeeded' state (stale/failed)
BlueprintResources
| where type =~ 'microsoft.blueprint/blueprintassignments'
| where tostring(properties.provisioningState) !~ 'succeeded'
| project subscriptionId, name, state=tostring(properties.provisioningState)

// 3. Resources locked by blueprint assignments (DoNotDelete or ReadOnly)
// These resources will lose lock protection after July 11, 2026 retirement
BlueprintResources
| where type =~ 'microsoft.blueprint/blueprintassignments'
| where tostring(properties.locks.mode) in ('AllResourcesDoNotDelete', 'AllResourcesReadOnly')
| project subscriptionId, name, lockMode=tostring(properties.locks.mode)
| extend MigrationNote = 'Locks removed at retirement — migrate to Deployment Stack denySettings'
Common Blueprint Mistakes
Mistake
Consequence
Fix
Storing the blueprint definition at subscription scope instead of MG scope
The definition is only visible to that subscription; it cannot be assigned to other subscriptions
Create blueprint definitions at a management group scope (--management-group); use targetScope: subscription for what the artifacts deploy
Not publishing before assigning
Draft blueprints cannot be assigned — the assignment CLI call returns an error referencing the unpublished state
Always run az blueprint publish with an explicit --version before az blueprint assignment create
Using parameter names that conflict with ARM template built-in functions
Parameter names like 'location' conflict with ARM's [location()] function and cause template evaluation errors
Prefix blueprint parameters: subscriptionLocation instead of location, resourceGroupName instead of name
Setting lock-mode AllResourcesReadOnly on a subscription with active workloads
ReadOnly locks prevent updates to all blueprint-managed resources — deployments fail silently until the lock is removed
Use AllResourcesDoNotDelete for landing zone protection; reserve ReadOnly only for compliance-frozen environments
Creating new blueprint assignments after July 11, 2026
New assignments on retired service — behavior undefined and unsupported
Use az stack sub create (Deployment Stacks) for all new environment provisioning
Not migrating before the retirement date
Blueprint locks on resources are released at retirement; resources can be deleted or modified by anyone with RBAC access
Complete the Deployment Stack migration before July 11, 2026; validate denySettings protect the same resources
Summary

Blueprint definitions combine resource groups, ARM templates, policy assignments, and role assignments into versioned deployable units. Definitions live at management group scope and are assigned to target subscriptions via az blueprint assignment create with a version reference, parameter values, and lock mode. Azure Blueprints retired July 11, 2026 — existing assignments continue running but lose enforcement. Migrate to Deployment Stacks using az stack sub create with denySettings before the retirement date to preserve resource protection.
